title: Crepinia psilophylla
intro: Crepinia psilophylla, also known as Kavalam, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, classified under the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Crepinia psilophylla has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Crepinia psilophylla is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used to make a topical paste for wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Crepinia psilophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is often found in dense forests. Within the Apocynaceae family, Crepinia psilophylla is further classified as a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The exact classification of Crepinia psilophylla may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a species within the Apocynaceae family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
